name: Report Formatter & Sealer
description: Formats ReviewReport into three output formats: JSON (validates against schema), Markdown (human-readable with severity badges), GitHub-compatible PR comment (collapsible sections per stage). Deterministic output: injectable timestamps, sorted keys, canonical serialization. Applies Tessera seal to final report (hash-chain proof over content). Includes verify function that confirms seal integrity and detects post-seal modification. Emits report.sealed Chronicler event via injectable callback. Module constant PACT_COMPONENT = 'reporter' for Sentinel attribution.

types.name: OutputFormat
types.description: Supported report output formats.

types.name: SealVerificationStatus
types.description: Outcome status of a seal verification check. StrEnum in implementation.

types.name: FormattedReport

fields.name: content
fields.description: The fully rendered report string in the requested output format.

fields.name: output_format
fields.description: Which format was used to render the report.

fields.name: report_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the source ReviewReport.

fields.name: is_sealed
fields.description: Whether the source ReviewReport had a valid TesseraSeal attached at render time.

fields.name: character_count
fields.description: Length of the content string in characters.

fields.name: truncated
fields.description: True if content was truncated (only applicable to github format at 65000 char limit).

fields.name: rendered_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the report was rendered.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Additional metadata including pact_component attribution key and any format-specific info.

types.description: Component-local frozen Pydantic model containing the rendered report string, seal status, and metadata. Immutable (frozen=True).

types.name: SealVerificationResult

fields.name: status
fields.description: Overall verification outcome.

fields.name: valid
fields.description: Convenience boolean: True iff status == 'valid'.

fields.name: content_hash_match
fields.description: Whether the recomputed content_hash matches the seal's content_hash.

fields.name: chain_hash_match
fields.description: Whether the recomputed chain_hash matches the seal's chain_hash.

fields.name: expected_content_hash
fields.description: The content_hash stored in the seal. Empty string if seal is missing.

fields.name: actual_content_hash
fields.description: The content_hash recomputed from the report content. Empty string if computation failed.

fields.name: expected_chain_hash
fields.description: The chain_hash stored in the seal. Empty string if seal is missing.

fields.name: actual_chain_hash
fields.description: The chain_hash recomputed from content_hash and previous_hash. Empty string if computation failed.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Human-readable error message if verification failed. Empty string on success.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model containing the full result of a seal integrity verification. Immutable (frozen=True).

types.name: ReportId

validators.error_message: ReportId must be a non-empty string.

types.description: Opaque unique identifier for a ReviewReport. Non-empty string.

types.name: Iso8601Timestamp

validators.error_message: Timestamp must be ISO 8601 UTC format ending in 'Z', e.g. '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'.

types.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp string ending in 'Z'. Used for deterministic injectable timestamps.

types.name: Sha256Hex

validators.error_message: SHA-256 hex must be exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, or empty string.

types.description: A SHA-256 hash value represented as a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string. May be empty string when hash is unavailable (e.g. missing seal).

types.name: SealerId

validators.error_message: SealerId must be a non-empty string.

types.description: Identifier of the entity (component or operator) applying the Tessera seal. Non-empty string.

types.name: ChroniclerCallback
types.description: Injectable callback type: Callable[[ChroniclerEvent], None] | None. When provided, called fire-and-forget (wrapped in try/except) after sealing to emit report.sealed events. None means no event emission.

types.name: SealChainStoreProtocol

fields.name: get_previous_hash
fields.description: Async method signature: async def get_previous_hash() -> str | None. Returns the most recent chain_hash from the seal chain store, or None if the chain is empty (GENESIS case).

fields.name: append_seal
fields.description: Async method signature: async def append_seal(seal: TesseraSeal) -> None. Appends a completed seal record to the chain store.

types.description: Protocol (runtime_checkable) abstracting seal chain persistence. Implementations read/write seal chain data (e.g. JSON lines file). Keeps file I/O out of the formatter for pure-logic testing per SOPs.

types.name: GitHubTruncationNotice
types.description:
Constant string appended when GitHub format output exceeds 65000 characters. Value: '

---
⚠️ *Report truncated. Full report available in JSON format.*'

types.name: GithubCharLimit

validators.error_message: GitHub character limit is fixed at 65000.

types.description: Maximum character count for GitHub PR comment format. Constant value: 65000.

types.name: TesseraSeal

fields.name: content_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON content that was sealed.

fields.name: previous_hash
fields.description: Hash of the previous seal in the chain (null for first seal).

fields.name: chain_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of (content_hash + previous_hash), forming the chain link.

fields.name: sealed_at
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the seal was created.

fields.name: sealer_id
fields.description: Identifier of the entity that created this seal.

types.description: A hash-chain tamper-proof seal over serialized content, verifiable independently.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Severity level of a review finding, ordered from most to least critical.

types.name: ReviewStage
types.description: Named stage in the review circuit pipeline.

functions.name: canonicalize
functions.description: Pure function: produces the canonical byte representation of a ReviewReport for hashing. Creates a seal-excluded copy of the report (seal=None), calls model_dump(mode='json'), then serializes with json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8'). This is the single source of truth for content hashing — both seal_report and verify_seal must use this function. No side effects.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to canonicalize. If it has a seal attached, the seal field is set to None before serialization.

error_cases.name: serialization_failure
error_cases.condition: The report contains data that cannot be serialized to JSON via model_dump(mode='json')

error_data.report_id: The report_id of the failing report

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance (Pydantic-validated)
postconditions: Return value is valid UTF-8 encoded JSON bytes
Return value is deterministic: same report content always produces identical bytes
Seal field is excluded from canonical representation (set to None before dump)
JSON keys are sorted lexicographically at all nesting levels
No whitespace padding in output (compact separators (',', ':'))
Calling canonicalize twice on the same report yields byte-identical output

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the total size of the report model graph

functions.name: seal_report
functions.description: Applies a Tessera seal to a ReviewReport. Computes content_hash as SHA-256 of canonicalize(report), retrieves previous_hash from the SealChainStoreProtocol (or uses the explicit previous_hash override), computes chain_hash as SHA-256 of (content_hash + '|' + (previous_hash or 'GENESIS')). Returns a new ReviewReport (via model_copy) with the TesseraSeal attached. Appends the seal to the chain store. Emits report.sealed ChroniclerEvent via the injectable callback if provided (fire-and-forget). Timestamp is injectable for deterministic testing.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to seal. Must not already have a seal attached.

inputs.name: chain_store
inputs.description: Seal chain store for retrieving previous_hash and persisting the new seal.

inputs.name: sealer_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the entity applying the seal. Included in the TesseraSeal.

inputs.name: timestamp
inputs.description: Optional injectable timestamp. If not provided (None), uses datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z'.

inputs.name: chronicler_callback
inputs.description: Optional callback for emitting report.sealed ChroniclerEvent. If None, no event is emitted. Errors in callback are caught and suppressed (fire-and-forget).

error_cases.name: already_sealed
error_cases.condition: report.seal is not None — the report already has a seal attached

error_cases.name: chain_store_read_failure
error_cases.condition: chain_store.get_previous_hash() raises an exception (e.g. file not found, permission denied)

error_cases.name: chain_store_write_failure
error_cases.condition: chain_store.append_seal() raises an exception (e.g. disk full, permission denied)

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
report.seal is None (report is not already sealed)
postconditions: Returned ReviewReport is a new instance (original is not mutated, frozen model)
Returned report.seal is a valid TesseraSeal with non-empty content_hash, chain_hash, sealed_at, and sealer_id
content_hash == SHA-256(canonicalize(original_report))
chain_hash == SHA-256(content_hash + '|' + (previous_hash or 'GENESIS'))
Seal has been appended to chain_store via append_seal
All fields of the returned report except seal are identical to the input report
If chronicler_callback was provided, report.sealed event was attempted (failure does not affect return value)
side_effects: Appends seal record to SealChainStoreProtocol (file I/O via chain store)
Optionally emits report.sealed ChroniclerEvent via callback (fire-and-forget)

structured_side_effects.description: Appends the completed TesseraSeal to the chain store via append_seal(). Actual I/O is delegated to the chain store implementation.
Emits report.sealed ChroniclerEvent via injectable callback. Fire-and-forget: exceptions are caught and suppressed.

functions.name: verify_seal
functions.description: Pure verification function: recomputes content_hash from the report via canonicalize(), recomputes chain_hash from content_hash and the seal's previous_hash, and compares both against the values stored in the seal. Returns a SealVerificationResult with match booleans and expected/actual hash values. Does not perform any I/O or mutate state. Does not access the chain store — verification is self-contained using only the report and its embedded seal.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport with TesseraSeal to verify.

error_cases.name: missing_seal
error_cases.condition: report.seal is None — no seal is attached to verify

error_data.error: Failed to canonicalize report for verification

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
postconditions: If report.seal is None, returns SealVerificationResult with status='missing_seal', valid=False
If content_hash matches and chain_hash matches, returns status='valid', valid=True
If content_hash does not match, returns status='invalid_content_hash', valid=False
If content_hash matches but chain_hash does not match, returns status='invalid_chain_hash', valid=False
expected_content_hash and expected_chain_hash are taken from the seal
actual_content_hash and actual_chain_hash are freshly recomputed
Function never raises exceptions — all errors are captured in the error field with status='verification_error'

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects. Returns errors as data, never raises.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the total size of the report model graph

functions.name: format_report
functions.description: Renders a ReviewReport into the specified OutputFormat. For JSON: canonical serialization with 2-space indent for readability (distinct from the compact canonical form used for hashing). For Markdown: severity emoji badges (🔴 critical, 🟠 high, 🟡 medium, 🔵 low, ⚪ info), findings grouped by ReviewStage, summary stats table. For GitHub: collapsible <details> sections per stage, truncation at 65000 characters with truncation notice appended. Timestamp is injectable for deterministic output. Returns a FormattedReport frozen model.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to format. May or may not have a seal attached.

inputs.name: output_format
inputs.description: The desired output format: json, markdown, or github.

inputs.name: timestamp
inputs.description: Optional injectable timestamp for the rendered_at field. If not provided (None), uses datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z'.

error_cases.name: unsupported_format
error_cases.condition: output_format is not one of the supported OutputFormat variants

error_cases.name: rendering_failure
error_cases.condition: An unexpected error occurs during rendering (e.g. malformed report data that passes validation but causes template errors)

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
output_format is a valid OutputFormat variant
postconditions: Returned FormattedReport.content is a non-empty string in the requested format
Returned FormattedReport.output_format matches the requested output_format
Returned FormattedReport.report_id matches report.report_id
Returned FormattedReport.is_sealed reflects whether report.seal is not None
Returned FormattedReport.character_count == len(FormattedReport.content)
For github format: if character_count > 65000, content is truncated and truncated=True
For json and markdown formats: truncated is always False
Returned FormattedReport.metadata contains key 'pact_component' with value 'reporter'
Output is deterministic: same inputs with same timestamp produce identical output
For json format: output is valid JSON parseable by json.loads()
For markdown format: output contains severity emoji badges for each finding
For github format: output contains <details> sections for each stage with findings

structured_side_effects.description: Pure rendering function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of findings in the report

functions.name: render_json
functions.description: Internal renderer: serializes the ReviewReport to a human-readable JSON string with 2-space indentation, sorted keys, and ensure_ascii=False. This is the readable format (distinct from the compact canonical form used by canonicalize for hashing). Includes the seal in the output if present.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to render as JSON.

error_cases.name: json_serialization_failure
error_cases.condition: Report data cannot be serialized to JSON

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
postconditions: Output is valid JSON parseable by json.loads()
Output uses 2-space indentation
JSON keys are sorted lexicographically at all nesting levels
Output includes seal data if report.seal is not None

functions.name: render_markdown
functions.description: Internal renderer: produces a Markdown string with severity emoji badges (🔴 critical, 🟠 high, 🟡 medium, 🔵 low, ⚪ info), findings grouped by ReviewStage, a summary statistics table with counts per severity, and seal status section if sealed. Deterministic ordering: stages sorted by enum order, findings within stage sorted by severity descending then by file path ascending.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to render as Markdown.

inputs.name: timestamp
inputs.description: Timestamp to include in the report header.

error_cases.name: markdown_rendering_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error during Markdown template rendering

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
postconditions: Output contains a top-level heading with report_id
Each finding has its severity emoji badge prefixed
Findings are grouped under stage-level headings
A summary table lists finding counts per severity level
If report.seal is not None, a seal status section is included

functions.name: render_github
functions.description: Internal renderer: produces a GitHub-compatible PR comment with collapsible <details> sections per ReviewStage, severity emoji badges, finding details, and a summary header. Truncates to 65000 characters if the output exceeds the GitHub comment size limit, appending a truncation notice. Deterministic ordering: same as render_markdown.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The ReviewReport to render as a GitHub PR comment.

inputs.name: timestamp
inputs.description: Timestamp to include in the report header.

error_cases.name: github_rendering_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error during GitHub format rendering

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
postconditions: Output contains <details> and <summary> tags for each stage with findings
Each finding has its severity emoji badge
If len(output) > 65000, output is truncated to 65000 chars and truncation notice is appended
If report has no findings for a stage, that stage section is omitted
Output is valid GitHub-flavored Markdown

invariants: PACT_COMPONENT module constant is always 'reporter' and is included in all ChroniclerEvent payloads and FormattedReport.metadata
canonicalize is the single source of truth for content hashing: both seal_report and verify_seal use canonicalize to compute content_hash
Seal content_hash is always SHA-256 of canonicalize(report_with_seal_set_to_None)
Seal chain_hash is always SHA-256 of (content_hash + '|' + (previous_hash or 'GENESIS'))
All hash values are lowercase hex-encoded 64-character strings
All ReviewReport instances are frozen (immutable): seal_report returns a new instance via model_copy, never mutates the input
verify_seal never raises exceptions: all error states are captured in the return value
Injectable timestamps: all functions that produce timestamps accept an optional timestamp parameter; if None, use datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z'
GitHub format output never exceeds 65000 characters (truncation with notice is applied)
JSON format output from format_report uses 2-space indent (readable), while canonicalize uses compact separators (for hashing)
ChroniclerCallback errors are always caught and suppressed (fire-and-forget pattern)
Determinism: identical inputs with identical injectable timestamps produce byte-identical outputs across all format_report and canonicalize calls

data_access.rationale: Reads ReviewReport objects which contain code review findings including file paths, line numbers, code snippets, and reviewer assessments (INTERNAL classification — no PII, but proprietary source code context). Writes seal chain records (content hashes and chain hashes) to the SealChainStoreProtocol which persists to a local JSON lines file. Emits ChroniclerEvent payloads containing report metadata (report_id, seal hashes, sealer_id) but no source code content.

side_effects.rationale: Appends completed TesseraSeal records to the seal chain store (JSON lines file) for hash-chain integrity verification. Contains only cryptographic hashes and metadata, no source code.
Emits report.sealed ChroniclerEvent via injectable callback after sealing. Contains report metadata and seal hashes for audit trail. Fire-and-forget: callback errors are suppressed.

authority.rationale: This component is the sole authority for: (1) report.formatting — canonical serialization and all three output format renderings of ReviewReport; (2) report.sealing — Tessera seal computation (content_hash and chain_hash) and seal chain management; (3) report.seal_verification — seal integrity verification logic. No other component may independently format reports, compute seals, or verify seal integrity.
